title: hibbertia sericea var. cinerea
intro: Hibbertia sericea var. cinerea, also known as the Grey Guinea Flower, is a flowering plant native to Australia, characterized by its grey, hairy leaves and white or yellow flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine, particularly for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and the treatment of skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The health benefits of the Grey Guinea Flower include its ability to soothe and calm the skin, reduce inflammation, and promote the healing of wounds. In herbal preparations, the plant can be made into a tea or infused oil to take advantage of its medicinal properties, making it a popular choice for natural remedies and skincare products.
